pi_items.source: Task specification (task.json description and metadata). This is the policy name to be created in AttendHRM system.
Task description specifies 'Weekday Overtime Threshold: 8 hours (480 mins)'. 480 minutes equals 8 hours, which is a standard full-time workday threshold used across HR/payroll systems.
Task description specifies 'Weekday Rate Multiplier: 1.5x'. This is the standard time-and-a-half overtime rate used across HR and payroll systems globally.
Task description specifies 'Weekly Off/Rest Day Rate Multiplier: 2.0x'. This is the standard double-time rate for overtime on rest days/holidays in HR systems.
Task description specifies 'Rounding: Round to nearest 30 minutes'. 30-minute rounding is a standard practice in payroll systems.
Task description specifies 'Minimum Overtime: 30 minutes'. This minimum threshold is typical for OT accrual policies in HR systems.

Verified via setup_attendhrm.ps1 script (lines 369-374). The actual database used in environment initialization is DEMO.FDB (the demo/trial database that ships with AttendHRM) located at C:\Program Files (x86)\Attend HRM\Data\DEMO.FDB. The filename 'ABORABORAHRM.FDB' does not appear in any installation scripts, setup scripts, or task scripts in the codebase. The directory structure 'Lenvica\AttendHRM' does not match the actual installation path which is 'Attend HRM' (with a space).

Web search verified AttendHRM is a real product from Lenvica (https://lenvica.com/download-attendhrm/, https://lenvica.com/). Installation script (install_attendhrm.ps1 line 12) confirms 'AttendHRM (Lenvica HRMS)'. Web search indicates overtime policy configuration is a documented feature of AttendHRM (https://lenvica.com/portfolio/how-to-pay-your-employees-for-every-minute-of-overtime/).

Environment setup scripts analyzed: install_attendhrm.ps1 (lines 182-202) verifies Firebird service is bundled with AttendHRM and starts as a Windows service. setup_attendhrm.ps1 (lines 15-52) waits for Firebird service to be running. Lines 346-420 use Firebird's isql.exe interactive SQL tool with Firebird UPSERT syntax (UPDATE OR INSERT).

pi_items.verified_value: Bundled demo/trial database from AttendHRM installation
pi_items.source: setup_attendhrm.ps1 (line 370) loads DEMO.FDB which is the demo database included with AttendHRM software. This is not production data but rather test/trial data bundled with the product.

privileged_info_summary: The task requires configuring an overtime policy named 'Manufacturing OT Policy' in AttendHRM with specific parameters: 480-minute (8-hour) daily threshold, 1.5x weekday rate, 2.0x rest day rate, 30-minute rounding, and 30-minute minimum OT threshold. These are all standard HR/payroll overtime policy values and are correctly documented in the task description. AttendHRM is a verified legitimate HRMS (Human Resource Management System) software from Lenvica (https://lenvica.com) that uses Firebird as its database engine. The task operates on a synthetic demo database (DEMO.FDB) that ships with AttendHRM, not production data. However, the claimed database path is significantly incorrect: the metadata states 'C:\\Program Files\\Lenvica\\AttendHRM\\DATA\\ABORABORAHRM.FDB' but the actual database is located at 'C:\\Program Files (x86)\\Attend HRM\\Data\\DEMO.FDB'. The filename ABORABORAHRM.FDB does not exist in any setup or installation scripts examined. This database path discrepancy is consistent with previously validated tasks (configure_work_shift, manual_attendance_entry, add_branch, add_employee_dependent) which all document similar database path contradictions.
